title: Entity-Relationship (ER) Diagrams
summary: Design databases by identifying entities, attributes, and relationships using ER diagrams.
content_html:
An Entity-Relationship (ER) diagram is a visual blueprint for a database that shows entities, their attributes, and relationships.

Components

  • Entity: real-world object stored in the DB (rectangle)
  • Attribute: property of an entity (oval)
  • Relationship: association between entities (diamond)
  • Primary key attribute: underlined in diagram

Cardinality (Relationship Types)

  • One-to-One (1:1): one person has one passport
  • One-to-Many (1:M): one teacher teaches many students
  • Many-to-Many (M:N): students enroll in many courses; courses have many students

Weak Entity

A weak entity cannot be uniquely identified by its own attributes alone — it depends on a parent (strong) entity. Shown with double rectangles in ER diagram.

From ER to Tables

  • Each entity becomes a table
  • Each attribute becomes a column
  • 1:M relationships: add FK to the 'many' side
  • M:N relationships: create a junction/bridge table

exercises.title: Weak entity
exercises.prompt: A weak entity is one that:
options: Cannot be uniquely identified without its parent entity
exercises.answer: Cannot be uniquely identified without its parent entity

options: How many instances of one entity relate to another (1:1, 1:M, M:N)
